definitions.description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted

builderHint.searchHint: Pair with `@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent` for chatbot workflows. Reply delivery is controlled by `options.responseMode` — `streaming` (Agent streams directly to widget) is simplest and preferred. For `lastNode` mode, the workflow's last-executed node MUST output `{ output: '<reply>' }` — typically the Agent itself or a Set node re-shaping data; ending the chain with a Data Table insert, HTTP Request, or other side-effect node will fail. Put logging or persistence on a parallel branch, not inline after the Agent.

relatedNodes.relationHint: Main reply producer; use `responseMode: 'streaming'` so the Agent streams directly to the widget.

Append at the end of a `responseMode: 'lastNode'` chain to re-shape the last node's output into `{ output: '<reply text>' }` when the natural last step (e.g. a Data Table insert) doesn't produce chat-shaped data.
Required for `responseMode: 'responseNodes'`. Place inside the flow wherever you want to emit a reply chunk.

definitions.eventTriggerDescription: Waiting for you to submit the chat
definitions.activationMessage: You can now make calls to your production chat URL.

properties.name: public
properties.description: Whether the chat should be publicly available or only accessible through the manual chat interface

options.name: Hosted Chat
options.description: Chat on a page served by n8n

options.name: Embedded Chat
options.description: Chat through a widget embedded in another page, or by calling a webhook

properties.displayName: Chat will be live at the URL above once this workflow is published. Live executions will show up in the ‘executions’ tab
properties.name: hostedChatNotice

properties.displayName: Follow the instructions here to embed chat in a webpage (or just call the webhook URL at the top of this section). Chat will be live once you publish this workflow
properties.name: embeddedChatNotice

options.name: Basic Auth
options.description: Simple username and password (the same one for all users)

options.name: n8n User Auth
options.description: Require user to be logged in with their n8n account

builderHint.propertyHint: Default to 'none'. n8n exposes inbound trigger URLs publicly by design. Only select an authentication method when the user explicitly asks to authenticate inbound traffic.

properties.name: initialMessages
properties.default:
Hi there! 👋
My name is Nathan. How can I assist you today?
properties.description: Default messages shown at the start of the chat, one per line

properties.displayName: Make Available in n8n Chat Hub
properties.name: availableInChat
properties.description: Whether to make the agent available in n8n Chat Hub for n8n instance users to chat with

properties.displayName: Your Chat Trigger node is out of date. To update, delete this node and insert a new Chat Trigger node.
properties.name: availableInChatNotice

properties.displayName: Your n8n users will be able to use this agent in Chat once this workflow is published. Make sure to share this workflow with at least Project Chat User access to all users who should use it.
properties.name: availableInChatNotice
agentIcon
properties.description: The icon of the agent on n8n Chat

properties.name: agentName
properties.description: The name of the agent on n8n Chat. Name of the workflow is used if left empty.

properties.name: agentDescription
properties.description: The description of the agent on n8n Chat

properties.name: suggestedPrompts
properties.description: Suggested prompts shown to users in n8n Chat Hub to start a conversation with the agent

options.name: allowFileUploads
options.description: Whether to allow file uploads in the chat

options.name: allowedFilesMimeTypes
options.description: Allowed file types for upload. Comma-separated list of MIME types.

options.name: allowedOrigins
options.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed for cross-origin non-preflight requests. Use * (default) to allow all origins.

options.name: allowFileUploads
options.description: Whether to allow file uploads in the chat

options.name: allowedFilesMimeTypes
options.description: Allowed file types for upload. Comma-separated list of MIME types.

options.name: inputPlaceholder
options.description: Shown as placeholder text in the chat input field

options.name: loadPreviousSession
Off
options.description: Loading messages of previous session is turned off

options.name: From Memory
options.description: Load session messages from memory

options.name: Manually
options.description: Manually return messages of session
If loading messages of a previous session should be enabled

builderHint.propertyHint: This ONLY rehydrates the chat widget UI when the user reopens it — it does NOT give the Agent memory. The Agent gets memory from its own memory subnode regardless of this setting. Only set to 'memory' if the user wants the widget to restore visible history on reload; if so, you MUST also attach a memory subnode to this trigger (use the same memory node as the Agent so widget history matches what the Agent remembers). Otherwise leave as 'notSupported'.

options.displayName: Require Button Click to Start Chat
options.name: showWelcomeScreen
options.description: Whether to show the welcome screen at the start of the chat

options.name: getStarted
options.description: Shown as part of the welcome screen, in the middle of the chat window

options.name: subtitle
options.default: Start a chat. We're here to help you 24/7.
options.description: Shown at the top of the chat, under the title

options.name: title
options.description: Shown at the top of the chat

options.name: customCss
options.description: Override default styling of the public chat interface with CSS

options.name: responseMode
When Last Node Finishes
options.description: Returns data of the last-executed node

options.name: Using 'Respond to Webhook' Node
options.description: Response defined in that node
When and how to respond to the webhook

builderHint.propertyHint: 'streaming' (preferred for Agent-backed chats): the connected Agent streams its reply to the widget directly — no extra wiring. Place logging or side-effects on a PARALLEL branch off the trigger or Agent, never inline after the Agent. 'lastNode': the last-executed node's output is sent to the widget — that node MUST emit `{ output: '<reply text>' }` (typically the Agent itself, or a Set node re-shaping data). NEVER terminate the chain with a Data Table insert, HTTP Request, or other side-effect node — their output is not a chat reply and the widget will error. 'responseNodes' / 'responseNode': requires explicit response nodes inside the flow (`@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chat` for chat-hub mode, `n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook` for webhook mode).

options.name: autoSaveHighlightedData
options.description: Whether to automatically save highlighted data. This data can then be used to filter executions in the Executions view. Available on Pro and Enterprise plans in n8n Cloud, and on Enterprise or registered Community Edition for self-hosted. Defaults to true.

options.name: allowedOrigins
options.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed for cross-origin non-preflight requests. Use * (default) to allow all origins.

options.name: allowFileUploads
options.description: Whether to allow file uploads in the chat

options.name: allowedFilesMimeTypes
options.description: Allowed file types for upload. Comma-separated list of MIME types.

options.name: inputPlaceholder
options.description: Shown as placeholder text in the chat input field

options.description: Loading messages of previous session is turned off
